documented_expected_behaviour: Break the statement into outcomes, priorities, scope, and trade-offs.
Force prioritisation rather than recording vague adjectives.
initial_manager_statement: I need a superstar who can do a bit of everything.

turns.manager_message: I need a superstar who can do a bit of everything.

next_question.question: What are the three most important growth responsibilities or outcomes this Head of Growth must own?

turns.manager_message: Honestly, just whoever can jump in and handle whatever the business throws at them - sales, marketing, ops, whatever's on fire that week. I trust them to figure out the priorities themselves.

next_question.question: What are the three most important ongoing responsibilities this Head of Growth must own, rather than ad hoc support areas?

turns.manager_message: My single most important priority is growing new-customer revenue by $2M this quarter, measured as the total value of new contracts signed with net-new logos.

next_question.question: What specific responsibilities and levers will this role own to deliver the $2M net-new contract target?

turns.manager_message: This role directly owns outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing to hit that target.

next_question.question: What other growth responsibilities, if any, are ongoing priorities beyond outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing?

turns.manager_message: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, owning the onboarding email sequence and product walkthrough end-to-end.

next_question.question: What is the measurement definition and reporting period for week-one onboarding drop-off?

rejected_turns.note: Recorded live OpenRouter rejection. See docs/DECISIONS_AND_LESSONS.md entry 2026-07-24 'Compound prioritised answers can trip the must-have/ambiguity overlap guard'.
rejected_turns.manager_message: Fine, if I have to pick three: (1) hit a $2M quarterly growth target across new-customer revenue, (2) fix our onboarding funnel where we lose 40% of signups in week one, and (3) represent growth in board meetings when I can't attend. Everything else is secondary.

rejected_turns.note: A second, differently-worded compound answer, still fully specified with measurement definitions, triggers the same guard because the model quotes the entire multi-clause sentence as source_statement for both each must_have requirement and its own follow-up ambiguity about delivery scope.
rejected_turns.manager_message: Top priority: grow new-customer revenue by $2M this quarter, measured as total value of new contracts signed. Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, measured from signup to completed first purchase. Third priority: represent growth at board meetings when I'm travelling, with authority to answer questions but not commit new spend.

incremental_requirements.name: New-customer revenue growth
incremental_requirements.description: Grow new-customer revenue by $2M during the quarter, measured as the total value of new contracts signed.
incremental_requirements.rationale: The manager labels this as the top priority and provides a target, timeframe, and measurement method.
incremental_requirements.source_statement: Top priority: grow new-customer revenue by $2M this quarter, measured as total value of new contracts signed.

incremental_requirements.name: Week-one onboarding drop-off reduction
incremental_requirements.description: Reduce week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, measured from signup to completed first purchase.
incremental_requirements.rationale: The manager labels this as the second priority and provides a baseline, target, and measurement definition.
incremental_requirements.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, measured from signup to completed first purchase.

incremental_requirements.name: Board-meeting representation
incremental_requirements.description: Represent growth at board meetings when the manager is travelling, with authority to answer questions but not commit new spend.
incremental_requirements.rationale: The manager explicitly identifies this as the third priority and states the authority boundary.
incremental_requirements.source_statement: Third priority: represent growth at board meetings when I'm travelling, with authority to answer questions but not commit new spend.

ambiguities.description: The specific actions, channels, and responsibilities through which the role is expected to achieve the $2M new-customer revenue target are not stated.
ambiguities.source_statement: Top priority: grow new-customer revenue by $2M this quarter, measured as total value of new contracts signed.
ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: The revenue outcome and measurement are defined, but the role's delivery scope is needed to establish accountable responsibilities.

ambiguities.description: The specific ownership and interventions expected to reduce onboarding drop-off are not stated.
ambiguities.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, measured from signup to completed first purchase.
ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: The metric definition and target are defined, but the role's scope across onboarding, product, customer experience, or other work is not.

ambiguities.description: The expected frequency of board-meeting representation and whether it applies to all board meetings during the manager's travel are not specified.
ambiguities.source_statement: Third priority: represent growth at board meetings when I'm travelling, with authority to answer questions but not commit new spend.
ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: The trigger is stated generally, but the operational expectation for coverage is not defined.

basic_info.title: Head of Growth
basic_info.initial_manager_statement: I need a superstar who can do a bit of everything.

requirements.name: Cross-functional contribution across sales, marketing, and operations
requirements.description: Ability to step in across sales, marketing, and operations as business needs arise.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly identifies sales, marketing, and operations as areas the person may need to handle.
requirements.source_statement: Honestly, just whoever can jump in and handle whatever the business throws at them - sales, marketing, ops, whatever's on fire that week.

requirements.name: Independent prioritization
requirements.description: Ability to determine work priorities independently.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states that they expect the person to determine priorities themselves.
requirements.source_statement: I trust them to figure out the priorities themselves.

requirements.name: New-customer revenue growth target
requirements.description: Grow new-customer revenue by $2M within the quarter, measured by the total value of new contracts signed with net-new logos.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager identifies this as the single most important priority and provides both a target and measurement definition.
requirements.source_statement: My single most important priority is growing new-customer revenue by $2M this quarter, measured as the total value of new contracts signed with net-new logos.

requirements.name: Outbound sales ownership
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states that the role directly owns outbound sales.
requirements.source_statement: This role directly owns outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing to hit that target.

requirements.name: Partnership deal-sourcing ownership
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states that the role directly owns partnership deal-sourcing.
requirements.source_statement: This role directly owns outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing to hit that target.

requirements.name: Onboarding email sequence ownership
requirements.description: Own the onboarding email sequence end-to-end.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly assigns end-to-end ownership of the onboarding email sequence.
requirements.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, owning the onboarding email sequence and product walkthrough end-to-end.

requirements.name: Product walkthrough ownership
requirements.business_rationale: The manager explicitly assigns end-to-end ownership of the product walkthrough.
requirements.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, owning the onboarding email sequence and product walkthrough end-to-end.

requirements.name: Week-one onboarding drop-off reduction target
requirements.description: Reduce week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states this as the role's second priority with a defined current rate and target threshold.
requirements.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, owning the onboarding email sequence and product walkthrough end-to-end.

open_assumptions.statement: The manager appears to envision a role that can operate beyond a narrowly defined growth remit.
open_assumptions.source_statement: Honestly, just whoever can jump in and handle whatever the business throws at them - sales, marketing, ops, whatever's on fire that week.

open_assumptions.statement: Outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing are intended to contribute toward the previously identified new-customer revenue growth target.
open_assumptions.source_statement: This role directly owns outbound sales and partnership deal-sourcing to hit that target.

open_assumptions.statement: The stated 40% baseline and under-20% target appear to refer to the same measurement of week-one onboarding drop-off.
open_assumptions.source_statement: Second priority: cut week-one onboarding drop-off from 40% to under 20%, owning the onboarding email sequence and product walkthrough end-to-end.

open_ambiguities.description: “Do a bit of everything” does not identify which growth responsibilities fall within the role’s scope or which are priorities.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: I need a superstar who can do a bit of everything.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: The manager needs to specify the core responsibilities and expected breadth of ownership.

open_ambiguities.description: The role's actual scope and priority responsibilities are not defined: it is unclear which sales, marketing, operational, or other duties are expected, and whether these are ongoing accountabilities or occasional urgent support.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: Honestly, just whoever can jump in and handle whatever the business throws at them - sales, marketing, ops, whatever's on fire that week.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: Specific responsibilities and their relative priority are needed to define the role and evaluate relevant experience.

open_ambiguities.description: The degree of decision-making authority, available resources, and escalation expectations for independently setting priorities is not specified.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: I trust them to figure out the priorities themselves.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: These details determine what independent prioritization means in practice.

excessive_requirement_issues.message: 6 day-one must-haves are listed; the manager must explicitly identify the top five.

blockers: At least one success outcome is required.
At least one responsibility is required.
At least one assessment method is required.
A human decision owner is required.
